itinerary: A private car will pick you up from Titilaka Hotel to take you to the bus or train station in Puno.

A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and will transfer you to the Chauchilla Cemetery, located at about 30 kilometers from Nazca City, which is an important pre-Inca necropolis. Some sources relate it to the ancient Wari culture and others to the Nazca culture, which fluorished in the area between the 2nd century BC and the 9th century AD. The cemetery shows vestiges of the ancient civilization, as well as fragments of pottery, textiles and mummies. Continue the tour to the Cantalloc aqueducts located 4 km from the city of Nasca. These 46 aqueducts have spiral-shaped ventilation wells that are part of one of a system of aqueducts of the same type (called 'puquios') built by the Nasca people. These ensured the supply of water to the city of Nasca and the surrounding fields, which allowed the cultivation of cotton, beans, potatoes and corn in an arid region.

Mercado 28 is a gastronomic space designed as a place to go through the different stalls and get lost among aromas and flavours. It consists of 18 carefully selected proposals, which offer a variety of cuisine ranging from traditional Peruvian cuisine, Peruvian-Japanese fusion, Spanish, sweets, coffee and more. Mercado 28 has a privileged location in one of the most attractive areas of the city, in the district of Miraflores, and has options for breakfast, lunch or to enjoy a few drinks to end the day. Take advantage of your visit to Lima to the fullest by visiting Mercado 28 and delighting in the local flavors.

Learn about national cuisine differently: experimenting. Immerse yourself in the local cuisine with a participatory class in one of the restaurants with the best panoramic view of Limas bay and its well-known Costa Verde. Learn how to prepare a Pisco Sour, the symbolic drink of the country and cook the national flag dish: ceviche, a marinated raw fish or seafood plate cut into small pieces. The best of Peruvian cuisine in an amazing location. After the class, you will enjoy a delicious lunch.

Relax and admire the masterful color of the Piuray lagoon, immersed in the middle of the Sacred Valley. Listen to the noise of the wind, admire the immensity of the mountains that surround it and discover a side of tranquility, well-being and relaxation. In this environment, have an Andean buffet lunch with local ingredients cooked in a clay oven. Among them, taste a variety of potatoes, sweet potato, goose, corn, mushrooms, chili stuffed with cheese, Andean cheese gratin, fresh chicken, bananas, huancaina and uchucuta sauces accompanied by a glass of wine or beer, emollient or a hot drink.

Surrounded by an impressive landscape of high mountains and green fields; its strategic location makes it an ideal place for passengers visiting Maras, Moray and Chinchero. Attention is only available with a prior reservation, and behind closed doors, this is why it does not have signs on the facade or information on the Internet, which makes it an exclusive environment. Here, visitors can enjoy a set menu, which varies according to the season, the requirements or food restrictions of each of the guests. The ingredients come from a field of cultivation and an organic bio-garden located within the premises of the house to guarantee a unique culinary experience.

A transport service takes you from the hotel to Abra Corao, where you will receive the security information and the equipment necessary for the activity. The first stop in the cycling tour is Tambomachay, a construction that has fine stone finishings and high command of hydraulic engineering, with aqueducts that continue supplying clear water to this place. This archaeological complex is located eight kilometers away from Cusco City. Also known as the Inca Bath, Tambomachay was probably the leisure place for the Inca and his entourage. The construction seems to have a special interest in water, which it worshipped, and fine aqueducts fed on groundwater aquifers and neighboring hot springs. VIsit Puka Pukara (‘Red fortress’ in Quechua) an Inca military complex located on a prominent hill. Its dwellings squares, bathrooms, aqueducts, walls, and towers can still be appreciated. It is believed that it was used by the Inca entourage while the leader rested in Tambomachay. Continue to Qenqo, an archaeological complex for religious use where it is believed that the Incas practiced rituals related to agriculture. Then head towards the cusqueñas hills where is the fortress of Sacsayhuamán, whose imposing walls offer an impressive panoramic view of Cusco. Finish this exciting tour at the San Cristobal viewpoint. Be transferred back to your hotel at the end of the tour.

#1 During the morning you will be picked up from your hotel in Cusco and transferred to the hot springs of Lares, approximately 4 hours away. Marvel at the beautiful landscapes along the way and, at arrival, enter the hot springs to which medicinal properties are attributed. Then, enjoy lunch before starting the trek to the camp at Huacahuasi. Upon arrival at the camp, you will be received with coffee, and then dinner will be served. Finally, you will have free time to rest and continue the journey the next day. #2 Start the day with breakfast at the camp and then resume the trek leaving Huacahuasi towards Patacancha. This stretch, which rises to 4400 meters of altitude, allows you to see beautiful landscapes such as the Veronica Mountain and its surroundings. Lunch on the way and continue the trek down to the camp at Patacancha (3650 masl). Upon arrival at the camp, you will be received with coffee, and then dinner will be served. Finally, you will have free time to rest. # 3 Have breakfast at the camp and then continue the trek to the archaeological site of Pumamarca, where platforms, canals and various Inca constructions can be appreciated. Continue the hike towards Ollantaytambo and have lunch on the way. Briefly walk the streets of Ollantaytambo before boarding the van that will take you to your hotel in Cusco or the Sacred Valley.

Peruvian Experience offers the ideal space to live a participatory gastronomic adventure at all levels. The tour begins with the Dark Ride, where you will learn interactively, and through special effects, about the origins of Peruvian cuisine. You will continue to the cellar to learn everything about the Pisco-making process, and you will participate in a Pisco Sour master class in the theme bar set in the 1920s. Finally, enter The Taste of Peru zone, a recreation of a Peruvian market where you will taste the best of local cuisine in a 14-course meal. Here, the cooks prepare delicious dishes accompanied by a fun show of rhythm, colour and flavour.
